column1: # types.tsv — every C struct in Lua's headers → Rust struct + field types
# Field rows are indented in column 1 (two leading spaces) and give the field's Rust type in column 2.
# For unions, the Rust mapping is an enum; see PORT_STRATEGY §3 for the canonical variant lists.

column4: unsigned byte; pervasive (tag bits, marked, flags)
signed byte; used for Proto.lineinfo deltas
signed memory counter (LUAI_MEM → isize); GCdebt can go negative
u32 newtype; bytecode word; see lopcodes for encoding helpers
continuation context; ptrdiff_t by default; coroutine stub in Phase A–D
C-callable function; returns count of pushed values

column2: fn(&mut LuaState, status: i32, ctx: isize) -> Result<usize, LuaError>
column4: continuation for yield/resume; coroutine stub in Phase A–D
chunk loader callback; signature TBD when load/dofile lands (Phase B/C); likely `Box<dyn FnMut() -> Option<&[u8]>>`
chunk writer callback for ldump; signature TBD; likely `Box<dyn FnMut(&[u8]) -> Result<(), LuaError>>`
C allocator hook; not exposed in Rust-native API — Rust's allocator handles it
per-thread state; full struct mapped under State types
debug introspection record; full mapping deferred to Phase E
Rust enum per PORT_STRATEGY §3.2; replaces the {Value, tt_} struct
C union (gc/p/f/i/n); subsumed by enum payload; no standalone Rust type
collectable pointer; in Rust the enum variant carries the typed GcRef directly
light userdata pointer; only raw-ptr case allowed by PORTING.md §3.1
light C function; held by LuaValue::Function (LightC variant)
stack slot; wrapper around LuaValue + optional tbc linkage
tbc (to-be-closed) linked-list delta; stored in StackValue's tbc variant
pointer into stack → u32 newtype index; PORT_STRATEGY §3.5
pointer-or-offset union collapses to a single index; reallocation no longer requires the dual
intrusive common header replaced by trait + enum/Rc dispatch
intrusive list pointer; replaced by per-list `Vec<GcRef<T>>` on GlobalState (allgc, finobj, etc.)
type tag stored in C; in Rust the enum variant + concrete type carry it
GC color/age bits; Phase D only — Phase A–C ignores it
macro-expanded header in C; in Rust each Collectable carries needed fields directly
C-only union for safe cast_u; Rust uses typed GcRef<T> per object — no union needed
byte-string newtype wrapping Rc<[u8]>; never `String` or `&str` — PORT_STRATEGY §3.3
reserved-word index for short strings; "has-hash" flag for longs; kept as u8 field
0xFF sentinel for long strings; in Rust use `enum LuaStringRepr { Short(Rc<[u8]>), Long(Rc<[u8]>) }` or carry the byte directly
long-string length; for short strings, shrlen holds length
intrusive intern-table chain; replaced by HashSet-style StringPool on GlobalState
flexible array member in C; Rust uses inline `Rc<[u8]>` (cheap clone, shared)
hash table for interned short strings; lives on GlobalState
open-addressing/chained buckets in C; in Rust use HashSet<Rc<[u8]>> or HashMap<&[u8], GcRef<LuaString>>
full userdata; carries optional user values + raw byte payload
number of user values; in Rust covered by Vec::len of uv field
number of raw bytes; covered by `data: Box<[u8]>`.len()
intrusive gray-list link; replaced per PORT_STRATEGY §3.4
flexible array member of UValue; Rust uses inline Vec (or Box<[LuaValue]>)
no-user-values variant; C uses it for alignment math only — drop in Rust
C alignment-padding union over a TValue; in Rust just store `LuaValue` directly
hybrid array+hash; the workhorse Lua container
bitfield: which metamethods are absent + BITRAS (array-real-size flag); Tag-style newtype per PORTING.md §4.9
log2 of hash-bucket count; keep as u8 to preserve bit-trick `1 << lsizenode`
array-size hint OR real array size (gated by BITRAS bit in flags)
array part; Rust uses Vec (or Box<[LuaValue]> + separate len) — PERF(port) revisit in Phase B
hash part; sized 2^lsizenode; rehashed by luaH_resize
first-free-position cursor for hash chaining; index into node
intrusive gray-list link; replaced per PORT_STRATEGY §3.4
one hash-bucket entry (key, value, next-chain index)
value field; in C reached as `node->i_val` (proper TValue alias of the key_val/tt fields)
key's tag byte; merged with key_val into a single Rust LuaValue
key's Value union; merged with key_tt into a single Rust LuaValue
chaining offset within the node array (can be negative); keep as i32
C-only embedded struct enabling tight layout; Rust holds key+value+next as plain fields
stored as lu_byte in C; Rust uses bool (or keep u8 if bit-tricked)
C size companion to upvalues array; in Rust covered by Vec::len
covered by Vec::len of k field
covered by Vec::len of code field
covered by Vec::len of lineinfo field
covered by Vec::len of p field
covered by Vec::len of locvars field
covered by Vec::len of abslineinfo field
intrusive gray-list link; replaced per PORT_STRATEGY §3.4
upvalue name (debug info; never null in practice)
whether captured from parent's stack (true) or parent's upvalue list (false)
debug info for one local variable's lifetime
first pc where variable is active
first pc where variable is dead
absolute-line record for fast line lookup
Rust enum over LClosure/CClosure (and LightC handled as a LuaValue::Function variant)
C macro header; Rust carries nupvalues implicitly via Vec::len
intrusive list link; replaced per PORT_STRATEGY §3.4
flexible array member in C; inline Vec in Rust (Box<[GcRef<UpVal>]> after freeze)
the C callback (Rust fn pointer; signature per primitive table)
flexible array member of plain LuaValues (not UpVal cells — C closures don't share)
Rust enum per PORT_STRATEGY §3.8: `enum UpVal { Open { thread, idx }, Closed(LuaValue) }`
C pointer to either a stack slot or to UpVal.u.value; Rust replaces with the enum discriminant
C-only stack-realloc shim; not needed when stack is indexed
intrusive open-upvalue list link; in Rust GlobalState/LuaState owns a `Vec<GcRef<UpVal>>` of open upvals
closed value payload; carried inside the Closed variant
per-call frame info; stored as `Vec<CallInfo>` on LuaState, addressed by CallInfoIdx
stack index of the called function
prev frame index (Option<CallInfoIdx> at boundary); PORTING.md §3.2
next frame index; Option<CallInfoIdx> at tail
saved program counter (was `const Instruction *savedpc`); offset into Proto.code
line/count hook trap flag (volatile sig_atomic_t in C)
continuation function for yieldable C calls; Phase E
C union {funcidx, nyield, nres, transferinfo}; Rust uses an enum or transient field per call phase
expected number of results from this call
bit-packed CIST_* flags; Tag(u16) newtype with accessor methods per PORTING.md §4.9
process-wide state shared by all threads; not exposed at API (accessed via state.global())
canonical nil; in Rust just `LuaValue::Nil` — may be removed
all collectable objects; Phase A–C this leaks (Rc cycles); Phase D adds proper tracing
C uses `GCObject **` cursor into allgc; replaced by `usize` cursor in Phase D
Phase D — generational cohort marker; replaced by index into allgc
threads with open upvalues; coroutine list (Phase E)
array of tag-method names indexed by TMS enum (ltm.h)
warning userdata; folded into the closure
thread status (LUA_OK / LUA_YIELD / LUA_ERR*); could become an enum
number of CallInfo entries in use; redundant once stack is `Vec<CallInfo>` — keep for parity initially
pointer to GlobalState in C; in Rust access via `state.global()` or an Rc<RefCell<GlobalState>>
end-of-stack sentinel; redundant once we use `Vec<StackValue>` — keep as `stack.len()` cap
the stack itself; reallocates → never hold &/&mut across mutating calls (PORT_STRATEGY §3.5)
open-upvalue list (was intrusive); ordered by stack position
to-be-closed variable slots; was intrusive list via StkIdRel + delta field on StackValue
intrusive threads-with-upvals link; replaced by membership in global_State.twups Vec
C longjmp recovery point; replaced by `Result<T, LuaError>` per PORT_STRATEGY §3.7
bottom CallInfo for first C→Lua entry; the Vec<CallInfo>'s element 0
packed (recursion_count | non_yieldable_count << 16); keep packed for parity
mask of which hook events are enabled (LUA_MASK*)
enum (TM_INDEX..TM_N); 24 metamethod slots; PORTING.md §3.2 references it
growable byte buffer used by the lexer for token text
growable byte storage; was `char *buffer` + manual realloc
chunk-reader stream wrapper; Phase A internal to the lexer crate
cursor index into current chunk (was `const char *p`); switch to index when chunk is owned
C-style userdata; absorbed by the Rust closure
back-pointer to lua_State; the lexer holds &mut LuaState directly

column1: # --- Parser/lexer-local types (lparser.h, llex.h — internal to lua-parse crate) ---

column4: parser-internal enum (VVOID..VVARARG); 20 variants; mirror as Rust enum
C union {ival, nval, strval, info, ind{idx,t}, var{ridx,vidx}}; Rust represents as enum payload tied to ExprKind or as a struct of all fields
C union of compile-time-const TValue and active-var record; in Rust use a struct with all fields (kind + ridx + pidx + name + optional const value)
compile-time constant value (only valid for RDKCTC)
C dynamic array; Rust uses Vec
parser-local mutable state (active vars + goto/label lists)
per-function compile-time state; internal to lua-parse
enclosing function (was raw pointer); use Box-chain or stack-of-frames
back-pointer; Rust passes &mut LexState into methods
chain of nested blocks; BlockCnt is defined in lparser.c (translation-unit-local)
# constants emitted (covered by Proto.k.len() during build — keep for parity)
index of first local var in dyd.actvar
index of first label in dyd.label
whether function needs CLOSE on return
defined in lparser.c (not header); ?internal-only?  — keep struct definition local to lua-parse crate
C union {r: lua_Number, i: lua_Integer, ts: TString*}; Rust uses an enum variant payload
int in C (chars + TK_* reserved values); Rust uses a TokenKind enum
back-pointer; Rust threads state via fn args
table that pins token strings to prevent GC during parse
parser dynamic data; owned by LexState during the parse
cached "_ENV" name (so we don't re-intern it constantly)
